A valid notebook purchase provides enough sheets of each color for n invitations.\n// Each invitation requires 2 red, 5 green, and 8 blue sheets.\n// Each notebook contains k sheets of a single color.\nghost predicate SufficientNotebooks(r: int, g: int, b: int, n: int, k: int)\n{\n  r >= 0 && g >= 0 && b >= 0 &&\n  r * k >= 2 * n &&\n  g * k >= 5 * n &&\n  b * k >= 8 * n\n}\n\n// m is the minimum non-negative integer such that m * k >= need:\n// the fewest k-sheet notebooks to obtain at least `need` sheets.\nghost predicate IsMinCover(m: int, need: int, k: int)\n  requires k >= 1\n{\n  m >= 0 &&\n  m * k >= need &&\n  (m == 0 || (m - 1) * k < need)\n}\n\n// The minimum total notebooks for n invitations with k-sheet notebooks.\n// Since colors are independent (notebooks are single-color), the global\n// minimum equals the sum of per-color minima.\nghost predicate IsMinTotalNotebooks(total: int, n: int, k: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n{\n  exists r: nat ::\n    exists g: nat ::\n      exists b: nat ::\n        IsMinCover(r, 2 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(g, 5 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(b, 8 * n, k) &&\n        SufficientNotebooks(r, g, b, n, k) &&\n        total == r + g + b\n}\n\nmethod PetyaAndOrigami(n: int, k: int) returns (notebooks: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n  ensures IsMinTotalNotebooks(notebooks, n, k)\n{\n  notebooks := ((n * 2 + k - 1) / k) + ((n * 5 + k - 1) / k) + ((n * 8 + k - 1) / k);\n}","numLines":43,"startLine":1,"totalLines":43}}},{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01UppsLCxowee61RAaDRQ6PZ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HZgquv2ZYQN9FUkJ5e7QSM","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/mchiesa/work/projects/2026-02-dafny-liftings/smt_analysis/results/0019_1080_A/verified.dfy","content":"// A valid notebook purchase provides enough sheets of each color for n invitations.\n// Each invitation requires 2 red, 5 green, and 8 blue sheets.\n// Each notebook contains k sheets of a single color.\nghost predicate SufficientNotebooks(r: int, g: int, b: int, n: int, k: int)\n{\n  r >= 0 && g >= 0 && b >= 0 &&\n  r * k >= 2 * n &&\n  g * k >= 5 * n &&\n  b * k >= 8 * n\n}\n\n// m is the minimum non-negative integer such that m * k >= need:\n// the fewest k-sheet notebooks to obtain at least `need` sheets.\nghost predicate IsMinCover(m: int, need: int, k: int)\n  requires k >= 1\n{\n  m >= 0 &&\n  m * k >= need &&\n  (m == 0 || (m - 1) * k < need)\n}\n\n// The minimum total notebooks for n invitations with k-sheet notebooks.\n// Since colors are independent (notebooks are single-color), the global\n// minimum equals the sum of per-color minima.\nghost predicate IsMinTotalNotebooks(total: int, n: int, k: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n{\n  exists r: nat ::\n    exists g: nat ::\n      exists b: nat ::\n        IsMinCover(r, 2 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(g, 5 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(b, 8 * n, k) &&\n        SufficientNotebooks(r, g, b, n, k) &&\n        total == r + g + b\n}\n\nlemma CeilDivIsMinCover(need: int, k: int)\n  requires k >= 1\n  requires need >= 0\n  ensures IsMinCover((need + k - 1) / k, need, k)\n{\n  var m := (need + k - 1) / k;\n  var rem := (need + k - 1) % k;\n  assert need + k - 1 == m * k + rem;\n  assert m * k == need + k - 1 - rem;\n  assert m * k >= need;\n  if m > 0 {\n    assert (m - 1) * k == need - 1 - rem;\n    assert (m - 1) * k < need;\n  }\n}\n\nmethod PetyaAndOrigami(n: int, k: int) returns (notebooks: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n  ensures IsMinTotalNotebooks(notebooks, n, k)\n{\n  var r := (n * 2 + k - 1) / k;\n  var g := (n * 5 + k - 1) / k;\n  var b := (n * 8 + k - 1) / k;\n\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(2 * n, k);\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(5 * n, k);\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(8 * n, k);\n\n  assert IsMinCover(r, 2 * n, k);\n  assert IsMinCover(g, 5 * n, k);\n  assert IsMinCover(b, 8 * n, k);\n  assert SufficientNotebooks(r, g, b, n, k);\n\n  notebooks := r + g + b;\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2927,"cache_read_input_tokens":18432,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2927},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb1f18c-7441-47a8-b028-7e21fd7a66eb","uuid":"c13dd69e-879d-4457-a920-ed1d36d209b7"},{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HZgquv2ZYQN9FUkJ5e7QSM","type":"tool_result","content":"File A valid notebook purchase provides enough sheets of each color for n invitations.\n// Each invitation requires 2 red, 5 green, and 8 blue sheets.\n// Each notebook contains k sheets of a single color.\nghost predicate SufficientNotebooks(r: int, g: int, b: int, n: int, k: int)\n{\n  r >= 0 && g >= 0 && b >= 0 &&\n  r * k >= 2 * n &&\n  g * k >= 5 * n &&\n  b * k >= 8 * n\n}\n\n// m is the minimum non-negative integer such that m * k >= need:\n// the fewest k-sheet notebooks to obtain at least `need` sheets.\nghost predicate IsMinCover(m: int, need: int, k: int)\n  requires k >= 1\n{\n  m >= 0 &&\n  m * k >= need &&\n  (m == 0 || (m - 1) * k < need)\n}\n\n// The minimum total notebooks for n invitations with k-sheet notebooks.\n// Since colors are independent (notebooks are single-color), the global\n// minimum equals the sum of per-color minima.\nghost predicate IsMinTotalNotebooks(total: int, n: int, k: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n{\n  exists r: nat ::\n    exists g: nat ::\n      exists b: nat ::\n        IsMinCover(r, 2 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(g, 5 * n, k) &&\n        IsMinCover(b, 8 * n, k) &&\n        SufficientNotebooks(r, g, b, n, k) &&\n        total == r + g + b\n}\n\nlemma CeilDivIsMinCover(need: int, k: int)\n  requires k >= 1\n  requires need >= 0\n  ensures IsMinCover((need + k - 1) / k, need, k)\n{\n  var m := (need + k - 1) / k;\n  var rem := (need + k - 1) % k;\n  assert need + k - 1 == m * k + rem;\n  assert m * k == need + k - 1 - rem;\n  assert m * k >= need;\n  if m > 0 {\n    assert (m - 1) * k == need - 1 - rem;\n    assert (m - 1) * k < need;\n  }\n}\n\nmethod PetyaAndOrigami(n: int, k: int) returns (notebooks: int)\n  requires n >= 1 && k >= 1\n  ensures IsMinTotalNotebooks(notebooks, n, k)\n{\n  var r := (n * 2 + k - 1) / k;\n  var g := (n * 5 + k - 1) / k;\n  var b := (n * 8 + k - 1) / k;\n\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(2 * n, k);\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(5 * n, k);\n  CeilDivIsMinCover(8 * n, k);\n\n  assert IsMinCover(r, 2 * n, k);\n  assert IsMinCover(g, 5 * n, k);\n  assert IsMinCover(b, 8 * n, k);\n  assert SufficientNotebooks(r, g, b, n, k);\n\n  notebooks := r + g +
